content.problemStatement:
Given two positive integers aaa and bbb, we define f(a,b)=lcm(a,b)−gcd⁡(a,b)f(a, b) = \text{lcm}(a, b) - \gcd(a, b)f(a,b)=lcm(a,b)−gcd(a,b), where lcm\text{lcm}lcm denotes the lowest common multiple and gcd⁡\gcdgcd denotes the greatest common divisor.

Chef has a positive integer NNN. He wonders, what is the maximum value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) over all pairs (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) such that aaa and bbb are positive integers, and a+b=Na + b = Na+b=N?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) that can be obtained while satisfying the conditions on aaa and bbb.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are two possible pairs of (a,b)(a, b)(a,b): (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1). For both of these pairs, we have lcm(a,b)=2\text{lcm}(a, b) = 2lcm(a,b)=2 and gcd⁡(a,b)=1\gcd(a, b) = 1gcd(a,b)=1, which gives f(a,b)=1f(a, b) = 1f(a,b)=1.
Test case 222: For (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), we have lcm(1,3)=3\text{lcm}(1, 3) = 3lcm(1,3)=3 and gcd⁡(1,3)=1\gcd(1, 3) = 1gcd(1,3)=1, giving f(1,3)=3−1=2f(1, 3) = 3 - 1 = 2f(1,3)=3−1=2. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) that can be obtained while satisfying the above conditions.
Test case 333: For (1,5)(1, 5)(1,5), we have lcm(1,5)=5\text{lcm}(1, 5) = 5lcm(1,5)=5 and gcd⁡(1,5)=1\gcd(1, 5) = 1gcd(1,5)=1, giving f(1,5)=5−1=4f(1, 5) = 5 - 1 = 4f(1,5)=5−1=4. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) that can be obtained while satisfying the above conditions.
